---
title: "¿Se puede conducir en Alemania con el carnet de conducir búlgaro? | La Verdad sobre la Homologación de Carnets TR→BG (2026)"
date: 2026-03-15
draft: false
images: ["img/bulgarehliyet/bulgar-ehliyeti-almanya.webp"]
featureimage: "img/bulgarehliyet/bulgar-ehliyeti-almanya.webp"
description: "¿Funciona cambiar el carnet de conducir turco por uno búlgaro en Alemania? Centros KAT, código TR, directiva de la UE y experiencia de primera mano. Respuestas claras para ciudadanos búlgaros."
tags: ["carnet búlgaro Alemania", "carnet turco Bulgaria", "homologación carnet Bulgaria", "KAT Sofia", "proceso carnet Bulgaria", "cambio carnet UE", "doble nacionalidad turco-búlgaro", "solución carnet Alemania", "Führerschein Bulgarien", "código TR carnet", "centro KAT Bulgaria", "carnet doble nacionalidad", "directiva UE carnet", "carnet extranjero Alemania", "carnet Sofía Bulgaria", "pasaporte turco en Bulgaria", "carnet Alemania 2025", "atajo carnet", "ciudadano búlgaro en Alemania", "EU Führerschein Türkei"]
categories: ["Alemania"]
summary: "Obtuve la ciudadanía búlgara y cambié mi carnet turco por uno búlgaro. Centros KAT en Sofía, requisito de traductor, 30 días de espera y la respuesta definitiva desde Alemania: no, no funciona. ¿Por qué? La directiva de la UE no lo permite."
translationKey: "post-bulgar-ehliyet"
---
| Dónde Obtener | Descripción | Requisito de Traducción/Apostilla |
| :------------------------------- | :----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  * **Red:** (B)
* **Yellow:** (Y)
* **Blue:** (U)

### **3. Giallo (Yellow)**
This is another color frequently associated with caution or attention. It's often used for temporary signs or to highlight specific areas.

### **4. Nero (Black)**
Black is typically used for informational signs, road markings, and the text on many traffic signs.

### **5. Bianco (White)**
White is the most common color for informational signs, destination signs, and the background for regulatory signs.

### **6. Verde (Green)**
Green in Italy is primarily used for directional signs indicating motorways (autostrade) and major roads.

### **7. Marrone (Brown)**
Brown is used to indicate tourist attractions, historical sites, and other points of interest.

### **When in doubt, remember the basics:**
*   **Red** usually means stop or prohibition.
*   **Blue** often indicates mandatory actions or information.
*   **Yellow** signals caution.
*   **Green** points you toward major routes.

Understanding these Italian road sign colors will not only help you navigate safely but also contribute to a smoother and more enjoyable driving experience in Italy. Buon viaggio!

---
